mnemonics: PIPE Your Commands Together Like a Water Pipe Carrying Data
keyPoints: Pipes (|) connect the standard output of one command to the standard input of another.
Redirection operators (> and <) connect commands to files, sending output to a file or reading input from a file.
Tee command (| tee) splits the output, sending it both to the next command and to a file or stdout.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that pipes only pass standard output, not standard error (stderr).
Overwriting a file accidentally by using a single > instead of appending with >>.
